steps.title: Identify the shift in conditions
steps.content: The first sentence explains the mechanism by which a firefly produces glow: drawing oxygen in triggers a chemical reaction that creates light. The blank then transitions to what happens in the opposite scenario—when oxygen stops being drawn in. The passage shifts between these two contrasting conditions.

steps.title: Match the transition to the opposing states
steps.content:
”By contrast,” correctly introduces the logical opposition between the state where oxygen is present (glow is produced) and the state where it is absent (glow ceases). This transition highlights how the second situation directly mirrors the reverse of the first, reinforcing the causal mechanism. The other options fail: “For instance,” would introduce an example of the mechanism (not a different condition); “Specifically,” would add detail to the glow-producing process (not reverse it); “In conclusion,” would suggest finishing the discussion (not comparing two scenarios).

That matches choice B — B is correct.
